reasoning: Currently, the CDC only has yellow fever travel health notices for four South American countries: Bolivia, Brazil, Colombia, and Peru. The recent PAHO epidemiological alert highlighted a surge of cases primarily in South America, and no current cases or outbreaks are reported in any North American (including Central American or Caribbean) country. Given the very short timeframe (5 weeks until June 1, 2025), the slow spread of such diseases across continents, and the historical rarity of yellow fever outbreaks requiring travel advisories in North America, a new travel health notice is highly unlikely.
